steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states Whitney's mission: championing avant-garde art and helping artists gain publicity. The second sentence describes a specific action she took. The transition must show that the action was taken in order to fulfill that mission.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To this end': means 'to accomplish this goal.' It correctly signals that the second sentence shows how she pursued the stated mission. ✓
  • Choice B — 'In other words': signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The second sentence is not a restatement — it describes a new, specific action. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In contrast': signals opposition between two ideas. There is no opposition here; both sentences support the same goal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Granted': signals a concession before a contradiction. Nothing is being conceded or contradicted. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
